title: A market vendor who knew me
summary: Describe a market vendor you visited often and tell a small story about one of your encounters.
topic: A market vendor who knew me
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a market vendor you visited so often that they got to know you. Describe what they sold and what they were like. Use past simple. Tell a small story about one visit that you remember, and explain why you miss going there.
hintStarters: When I lived in …, there was a vendor at the market who always…
Every Saturday morning, I went to … stall to buy…
The vendor remembered my name and always…
sampleText: When I was a student living in Bologna, there was a cheese vendor at the Saturday market whose name was Marco. His stall was easy to find because it had a red and white striped awning and always smelled of aged parmesan and fresh ricotta. I started going there because it was the cheapest stall in the market square, but I kept going back because of Marco himself. He was about sixty years old, had a thick grey moustache, and always wore the same dark blue apron. After a few visits, he started to recognise me and remembered that I always wanted a small piece of pecorino. One Saturday in November, I arrived at the market and his stall was closed. A sign said he was sick. I felt genuinely disappointed and bought my cheese from another stall, but it was not the same. The following week, Marco was back. He saw me coming from a distance and held up a wedge of pecorino before I even reached his table. He had set it aside for me because he knew I would come. He wrapped it in white paper and wrote the price with a black marker. I gave him the exact coins and he thanked me with a slight bow. I moved away from Bologna two years later and I still think about that market on Saturday mornings. Good vendors make a neighbourhood feel like home.

quầy hàng của ông đóng cửa
cảm thấy thực sự thất vọng

Good vendors make a neighbourhood feel like home

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for habitual and single past events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past simple cho cả thói quen trong quá khứ ('I started going there', 'he remembered') lẫn các sự kiện đơn lẻ ('One Saturday, his stall was closed'). Cả hai đều dùng cùng một thì, nhưng ngữ cảnh cho biết đó là thói quen hay sự kiện một lần.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple for both past habits ('I started going there', 'he remembered') and single events ('One Saturday, his stall was closed'). Both use the same tense; context tells you whether it was habitual or a one-time event.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi còn là sinh viên sống ở Bologna, có một người bán phô mai tại chợ thứ Bảy tên là Marco. Quầy của ông dễ tìm vì có mái che kẻ sọc đỏ trắng và luôn thoảng mùi parmesan lâu năm và ricotta tươi. Tôi bắt đầu đến đó vì đây là quầy rẻ nhất trong quảng trường chợ, nhưng tôi cứ quay lại vì chính bản thân Marco. Ông khoảng sáu mươi tuổi, có bộ ria xám dày, và luôn mặc cùng một chiếc tạp dề xanh đậm. Sau vài lần ghé thăm, ông bắt đầu nhận ra tôi và nhớ rằng tôi luôn muốn một miếng phô mai pecorino nhỏ. Một thứ Bảy tháng Mười một, tôi đến chợ và quầy của ông đóng cửa. Một tờ biển ghi rằng ông bị ốm. Tôi cảm thấy thực sự thất vọng và mua phô mai ở quầy khác, nhưng không giống như vậy. Tuần sau, Marco trở lại. Ông thấy tôi từ xa và cầm lên một miếng pecorino trước khi tôi kịp đến quầy. Ông đã để dành cho tôi vì biết tôi sẽ đến. Ông gói nó bằng giấy trắng và viết giá bằng bút đen. Tôi đưa cho ông đúng số tiền xu và ông cảm ơn tôi với một cái gật đầu nhẹ. Tôi rời Bologna hai năm sau và vẫn nghĩ về khu chợ đó vào những sáng thứ Bảy. Những người bán hàng tốt làm cho khu phố cảm thấy như nhà.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did Marco sell at the market?
mcQuestions.explanation: Marco was a cheese vendor — he sold parmesan, ricotta, and pecorino.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why did the writer first start going to Marco's stall?
options: Because it was the cheapest stall in the market
Because the cheese smelled the best
Because the stall was near the writer's home
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer says 'I started going there because it was the cheapest stall in the market square.'
mcQuestions.hint: Find the first reason the writer gives.

mcQuestions.prompt: What happened when Marco was sick one Saturday?
options: The stall was closed and the writer bought cheese elsewhere
The writer did not go to the market that day
Marco opened the stall but left early
mcQuestions.explanation: Marco's stall was closed with a sign saying he was sick, and the writer bought cheese from another stall.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentences about the November Saturday.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did Marco do when he saw the writer the following week?
options: He held up a piece of cheese before the writer reached his table
He asked why the writer came back
mcQuestions.explanation: Marco held up a wedge of pecorino before the writer even reached his table.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about what Marco did from a distance.

options: To keep something reserved for someone
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Set aside' means to keep or reserve something for a specific person or purpose.
mcQuestions.hint: Marco kept a piece specifically for the writer — he ___ it for the writer.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past simple for a past habit?
options: He always wore the same dark blue apron.
He was always wearing the same dark blue apron.
He always wears the same dark blue apron.
He was wore the same dark blue apron always.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple with 'always' is used for habitual actions in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'stall' mean in the context of a market?
options: A table or stand where goods are sold
A small shop with a door
A place where animals are kept
mcQuestions.explanation: A market stall is an outdoor stand or table where a vendor sells goods.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about the structure at an outdoor market where someone sells things.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'He wrapped it ___ white paper.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wrapped in' is the correct collocation — you wrap something in paper or cloth.

options: To know someone or something because you have seen them before
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Recognise' means to identify someone or something because you have seen them before.
mcQuestions.hint: Marco started to identify the writer as a familiar face.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the writer's conclusion about good vendors?
options: They make a neighbourhood feel like home
They are always the cheapest option
mcQuestions.explanation: The final sentence says: 'Good vendors make a neighbourhood feel like home.'
mcQuestions.hint: Read the last sentence of the passage.

mcQuestions.explanation: 'Genuinely' means truly or sincerely, with no pretence.
mcQuestions.hint: The writer felt truly — not just a little — disappointed.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think small local markets are important for communities? What do you think is lost when local market vendors are replaced by large supermarkets?
